about.title: About PlantDGD
about.subtitle: Your trusted wholesale partner for ornamental plants in Romania and Europe
about.story1: PlantDGD S.R.L. is a company specialized in wholesale ornamental plants. We supply professional landscapers, garden centers, municipalities and private companies in Romania with premium quality plants at competitive prices.
about.story2: We produce and deliver conifers, ornamental trees, shrubs, perennials and hedge plants on a commercial scale. We work with clients from all over Romania and Europe, offering comprehensive services from species selection consulting to specialized transport.
about.naturalDesc: All our plants are naturally grown without harmful chemicals. We respect the environment.
about.qualityDesc: We provide only the highest quality plants, carefully selected and professionally maintained.
about.reliabilityDesc: Our clients are our priority. We respect deadlines, quantities and agreed conditions.
about.sustainabilityDesc: We are committed to environmental protection and sustainable agricultural practices.
about.landscapersDesc: Wholesale plant delivery for landscaping projects of any scale.
about.gardenCentersDesc: Advantageous partnerships for garden centers throughout Romania and Europe.
about.municipalitiesDesc: Plants for public spaces, parks and urban planning at special prices.
about.developersDesc: Complete planting solutions for residential and commercial complexes.

footer.description: Your wholesale supplier of ornamental plants. Premium quality, competitive prices, delivery throughout Romania.

1.name: Emerald Green Arborvitae (Thuja occidentalis 'Smaragd')
1.description: Emerald Green Arborvitae (Thuja occidentalis 'Smaragd') is the most popular evergreen hedge conifer. Narrow, columnar form with vibrant green colour year-round. Available wholesale in large quantities.

2.name: English Yew (Taxus baccata)
2.description: English Yew (Taxus baccata) is one of the longest-lived conifers in Europe, excellent for topiary and formal hedging. Slow growing but exceptionally durable and decorative.

3.name: Austrian Pine (Pinus nigra)
3.description: Austrian Pine (Pinus nigra) is a robust, wind-resistant evergreen tree with characteristic dark green needles. Tolerates poor soils, drought and urban pollution – ideal for large-scale plantings.

4.name: Oriental Arborvitae (Platycladus orientalis)
4.description: Oriental Arborvitae (Platycladus orientalis) is an evergreen conifer with elegant, fan-shaped vertical sprays. More drought-tolerant than common thuja – ideal for warm, exposed sites.

5.name: Colorado Blue Spruce (Picea pungens 'Glauca')
5.description: Colorado Blue Spruce (Picea pungens 'Glauca') is a stunning evergreen with distinctive silvery-blue needles. A premium specimen tree for parks, public spaces and landscape focal points.

6.name: Creeping Juniper (Juniperus horizontalis)
6.description: Creeping Juniper (Juniperus horizontalis) is a low-growing, spreading groundcover conifer. Ideal for slope stabilisation, ground cover and rockery planting.

7.name: Indian Bean Tree (Catalpa bignonioides)
7.description: Indian Bean Tree (Catalpa bignonioides) is a bold ornamental tree with large tropical-looking leaves and white orchid-like flowers in summer. An impressive shade tree for parks and avenues.

8.name: Southern Magnolia (Magnolia grandiflora)
8.description: Southern Magnolia (Magnolia grandiflora) is a magnificent evergreen tree bearing enormous, fragrant white flowers in summer. A premium statement tree for high-end landscaping projects.

9.name: Prunus laurocerasus
9.description: Cherry Laurel (Prunus laurocerasus) is the most widely used evergreen hedging shrub in Europe. Dense, glossy foliage, fast-growing and tolerant of shade, frost and urban conditions.

10.name: Red Robin Photinia (Photinia × fraseri 'Red Robin')
10.description: Red Robin Photinia (Photinia × fraseri 'Red Robin') is a striking evergreen shrub with vivid red new growth in spring. Excellent as a hedge or specimen shrub in commercial and residential projects.

11.name: Chinese Fountain Grass (Pennisetum alopecuroides)
11.description: Chinese Fountain Grass (Pennisetum alopecuroides) is a graceful ornamental grass with arching foliage and fluffy bottle-brush flower heads. Ideal for naturalistic and contemporary landscape designs.

12.name: Bamboo (Phyllostachys / Fargesia)
12.description: Bamboo is a fast-growing plant that creates natural privacy screens and windbreaks. Available in clump-forming (Fargesia) and running (Phyllostachys) varieties for different applications.

13.name: Hydrangea (Hydrangea)
13.description: Hydrangea is a classic flowering shrub bearing large mophead or lacecap blooms in white, pink and blue. A versatile plant for commercial gardens, parks and public spaces.

14.name: Common Ivy (Hedera helix)
14.description: Common Ivy (Hedera helix) is a vigorous, self-clinging evergreen climber, ideal for covering walls, fences and bare structures. Provides year-round coverage with minimal maintenance.

15.name: Box-leaved Honeysuckle (Lonicera nitida)
15.description: Box-leaved Honeysuckle (Lonicera nitida) forms a dense, small-leaved evergreen hedge that responds excellently to clipping. Perfect for low formal hedges and edging in commercial landscapes.

16.name: Coral Bells (Heuchera)
16.description: Coral Bells (Heuchera) is a versatile perennial valued for its striking foliage in shades of burgundy, bronze, lime and silver. Provides colour and texture in borders throughout the year.

17.name: Rose of Sharon (Hibiscus syriacus)
17.description: Rose of Sharon (Hibiscus syriacus) is a hardy deciduous shrub flowering prolifically from July to October in shades of white, pink and violet. An excellent choice for mixed borders and hedging.

18.name: Spirea / Bridalwreath (Spiraea)
18.description: Spirea (Spiraea) is a flowering deciduous shrub producing masses of white or pink blossom in spring and summer. Low-maintenance and highly rewarding in borders and naturalistic plantings.

19.name: Standard Mugo Pine (Pinus mugo on stem)
19.description: Standard Mugo Pine (Pinus mugo) grafted onto a clear stem creates a compact, ball-headed specimen. An elegant focal point for entrance areas, patios and formal garden designs.

20.name: Peony (Paeonia)
20.description: Peony (Paeonia) is a long-lived herbaceous perennial producing large, fragrant blooms in white, pink and red each spring. Once established, it thrives for decades with minimal care.

21.name: Common Bugle (Ajuga reptans)
21.description: Common Bugle (Ajuga reptans) is a low-growing, spreading ground cover perennial with blue flower spikes in spring. Ideal for underplanting trees and shrubs in shaded areas.

22.name: False Goatsbeard (Astilbe)
22.description: False Goatsbeard (Astilbe) is an elegant moisture-loving perennial with feathery plumes in pink, red and white. Excellent for damp, shaded borders and waterside plantings.

23.name: Japanese Sedge 'Ice Dance' (Carex morrowii)
23.description: Japanese Sedge 'Ice Dance' (Carex morrowii) is a neat, semi-evergreen ornamental grass with green leaves edged in creamy white. Suitable for containers, rockeries and woodland gardens.

24.name: Blue Fescue (Festuca glauca)
24.description: Blue Fescue (Festuca glauca) is a compact ornamental grass with distinctive blue-grey tufted foliage. Drought-tolerant and ideal for rockeries, gravel gardens and contemporary landscapes.

25.name: Daylily (Hemerocallis)
25.description: Daylily (Hemerocallis) is a reliable, long-flowering perennial producing trumpet-shaped blooms in orange, yellow and red from June to August. Near-indestructible and suitable for mass planting.

26.name: Plantain Lily (Hosta)
26.description: Plantain Lily (Hosta) is the premier shade garden perennial, grown for its bold, decorative foliage in shades of blue, green and gold. An essential plant for woodland and shaded borders.

27.name: Bearded Iris (Iris germanica)
27.description: Bearded Iris (Iris germanica) is a classic cottage garden perennial bearing elegant flowers in purple, blue, white and yellow in late spring. Drought-tolerant and easy to grow.

28.name: Red Hot Poker (Kniphofia uvaria)
28.description: Red Hot Poker (Kniphofia uvaria) is a striking architectural perennial with vivid orange-red torch-like flower spikes. A dramatic focal point in sunny borders and contemporary gardens.

29.name: Monkey Grass (Ophiopogon japonicus)
29.description: Monkey Grass (Ophiopogon japonicus) is an evergreen, grass-like ground cover that forms dense, weed-suppressing carpets. Low-maintenance and suitable for shaded areas and underplanting.

30.name: Obedient Plant (Physostegia virginiana 'Alba')
30.description: Obedient Plant (Physostegia virginiana 'Alba') produces spikes of white tubular flowers in late summer. A vigorous, easy-to-grow perennial for naturalistic and informal border planting.

31.name: Stonecrop 'Angelina' (Sedum rupestre)
31.description: Stonecrop 'Angelina' (Sedum rupestre) is a succulent ground cover with golden-yellow needle-like leaves that turn vivid orange in autumn. Perfect for dry rockeries and green roofs.

32.name: Hybrid Stonecrop (Sedum hybridum)
32.description: Hybrid Stonecrop (Sedum hybridum) forms dense, evergreen mats of bright green succulent leaves. Drought-tolerant and ideal for large-scale ground cover and extensive green roof systems.

33.name: Rock Stonecrop 'Blue' (Sedum rupestre)
33.description: Rock Stonecrop 'Blue' (Sedum rupestre) is a low-growing succulent with attractive blue-grey foliage. Virtually indestructible and perfect for dry, sunny positions and wall crevices.

34.name: Watson's Stonecrop (Sedum)
34.description: Watson's Stonecrop is a decorative succulent perennial with attractive pink-green rosettes. Retains its colour and form throughout the growing season.

35.name: Ice Plant (Hylotelephium spectabile)
35.description: Ice Plant (Hylotelephium spectabile) is a robust late-season perennial with broad, fleshy leaves and large flat-topped pink flower heads in late summer. An excellent pollinator plant.

36.name: Lamb's Ear (Stachys byzantina)
36.description: Lamb's Ear (Stachys byzantina) is beloved for its thick, soft, silver-grey woolly leaves. An exceptional textural ground cover and edging plant for sunny, well-drained positions.

37.name: Olive Tree (Olea europaea)
37.description: Olive Tree (Olea europaea) is an iconic evergreen Mediterranean tree with distinctive silvery-green foliage. Long-lived and architectural – increasingly popular in northern European garden design.

38.name: Bonsai
38.description: Bonsai is the ancient Japanese art of cultivating miniature trees. Each piece is unique and handcrafted – an exceptional gift or statement decorative element for interiors and terraces.
